001package tools.PathplannerFlip;
002
003import java.io.File;
004import java.io.IOException;
005import java.util.Arrays;
006
007import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.node.ArrayNode;
008import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.node.ObjectNode;
009
010/**
011 *
012 *
013 * <h2>Flip autons of your choice across the y coordinate axis</h2>
014 *
015 * <p>
016 * To use, run:
017 *
018 * <pre>
019 * ./gradlew runPathplannerAutonFlip -Pargs="[target autons]"
020 * </pre>
021 * 
022 * Split each auton you want to flip with ", "
023 * 
024 * <p>
025 * <b>Example:</b>
026 *
027 * <pre>
028 * ./gradlew runPathplannerAutonFlip -Pargs="RB 1 Sweep Angled, Front Hub Shoot Preloads"
029 * </pre>
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * <b>Arguments:</b>
033 *
034 * <ul>
035 * <li><b>[target autons]</b>: The autons to flip over the y axis.
036 * </ul>
037 */
038public class PathplannerAutonFlip {
039    protected static final String AUTON_DIR = "./src/main/deploy/pathplanner/autos/";
040
041    public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException {
042        if (args.length <= 0) {
043            System.err.println("Missing path argument.");
044            return;
045        }
046        flipAutons(args[0].split(", "));
047    }
048
049    public static void flipAutons(String... autons) throws IOException {
050        for (String autonName : autons) {
051            final File autonFile = new File(AUTON_DIR + autonName + ".auto");
052            if (!PathplannerPathFlip.fileValid(autonFile)) {
053                System.err.println(autonName + " - is NOT a valid auton </3");
054                continue;
055            }
056
057            final ObjectNode autonObj = (ObjectNode) PathplannerPathFlip.mapper.readTree(autonFile);
058
059            ArrayNode commandArray = (ArrayNode) autonObj
060                .path("command")
061                .path("data")
062                .path("commands");
063
064            String[] pathNames = commandArray.valueStream()
065                .filter(command -> command.path("type").asText().equals("path"))
066                .map(command -> command.path("data").path("pathName").asText())
067                .toArray(String[]::new);
068
069            if (pathNames.length <= 0) {
070                System.err.println("The auton " + autonName + " has no path commands");
071                continue;
072            }
073
074            writeAuton(autonObj, autonName, PathplannerPathFlip.flipPaths(pathNames));
075        }
076    }
077
078    public static final ObjectNode blankPathObject = PathplannerPathFlip.mapper.createObjectNode()
079        .put("type", "path")
080        .set("data", PathplannerPathFlip.mapper.createObjectNode());
081
082    public static void writeAuton(ObjectNode autonObj, String name, String[] paths) throws IOException {
083        ObjectNode dataNode = (ObjectNode) autonObj
084            .path("command")
085            .path("data");
086        
087        ArrayNode flippedPaths = PathplannerPathFlip.mapper.createArrayNode();
088        Arrays.stream(paths)
089            .map(pathName -> {
090                ObjectNode pathObj = (ObjectNode) blankPathObject.deepCopy().path("data");
091                pathObj.put("pathName", pathName);
092                return (ObjectNode) blankPathObject.deepCopy().set("data", pathObj);
093            })
094            .forEach(flippedPaths::add);
095        dataNode.set("commands", flippedPaths);
096
097        PathplannerPathFlip.mapper
098            .writerWithDefaultPrettyPrinter()
099            .writeValue(new File(AUTON_DIR + name + " Flipped.auto"), autonObj);
100        System.out.println("Saved to " + name + " Flipped.auto");
101    }
102}
